title: fix: no-play argument for API client
body:
**Is this PR adding new feature or fix a BUG?**

Fix BUG.

**Is this pull request related to any issue? If yes, please link the issue.**

N/A

**Description**

Currently, `--play` has no effect because its default value is `True`. The parser does not recognize anything like `--play False`. It only detect whether the argument exists.

Therefore, we can use [BooleanOptionalAction](https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html#argparse.BooleanOptionalAction), which automatically add the `--no-play` option, so that the play argument can be set to `False`.

**Compatibility**

This change does not break the existing behaviors because it adds a new option.

files_modified.patch:
@@ -61,7 +61,7 @@ def parse_args():
     )
     parser.add_argument(
         "--play",
-        type=bool,
+        action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction,
         default=True,
         help="Whether to play audio after receiving data",
     )

steps.operation: Switch the `--play` CLI flag to use `argparse.BooleanOptionalAction` so argparse automatically supports both `--play` and `--no-play` while keeping the default as `True`.
steps.reason: Using `type=bool` does not allow users to pass `--play False` (argparse treats presence as truthy), so the flag could not be explicitly disabled; `BooleanOptionalAction` correctly models a boolean option and adds `--no-play` for setting it to `False`.
steps.side_effects: CLI behavior now includes an additional `--no-play` option and help text output changes accordingly; any scripts or documentation that enumerate available flags may need updating, though existing `--play` usage remains compatible.

step_plan.summary: Fixes the API client CLI so `--play` can be explicitly disabled by adding argparse’s `--no-play` counterpart via `BooleanOptionalAction`, while preserving the default behavior.
